strengths: Extensive TypeScript/Node.js and React production experience across multiple Berlin companies (Optilyz, RIDE, re:cinq)
Genuine fullstack breadth from databases to UI, plus recent LLM/MCP and Kubernetes exposure matching several nice-to-haves
Demonstrated clean-architecture and testing discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing workshops)
concerns: Severely overqualified: a Staff/Senior engineer with ~18 years of experience applying to an entry-level role poses high flight-risk, salary mismatch, and likely disengagement with junior-scope work
No PostgreSQL or Nest.js evidence — relational experience is listed only as generic SQL/MySQL, with primary depth in MongoDB and niche DLang tooling irrelevant to this stack
Currently in a career gap since October 2025 following a six-month stint, and no exposure to the renewable-energy/construction domain the platform serves

key_factors.factor: Retention risk (career gap, recent short tenure)

response.justification: While the candidate is technically capable well beyond the role's requirements, that is precisely the problem: this is an entry-level position designed for someone growing into responsibility, and hiring an 18-year veteran ex-Staff engineer into it invites boredom, compensation friction, and early attrition. The specific stack anchors of the role — PostgreSQL and Nest.js — are unevidenced, his recent trajectory shows a short six-month engagement followed by an ongoing career gap, and he brings no domain familiarity with energy consulting or renovation workflows. For a team seeking a committed, moldable junior-to-mid developer with high on-site presence, this profile is a weak fit.
